inputs.prompt: Announcement: A 6-part documentary series commissioned by Channel 4. Title: Breaking Ground. Format: 6 x 48-minute documentary series. Production company: Apex Documentary Films. Director: Thomas Kendrick. Subject: The series follows six families who have built their own homes from scratch, documenting the emotional, financial, and legal battles of self-build housing in the UK. Broadcast window: Spring 2027. Quote from director: 'Every house in this series is a story about what people are willing to risk for the life they want.'
Announcement: Award win. Title: Undercurrent. Format: Feature documentary. Production company: Riverside Productions. Award: Best Documentary at the Nordic Film Festival 2026.

Announcement: Co-production deal between a German and French production company for a new investigative documentary. Title: The Brussels Effect. Format: 3 x 52-minute documentary series. Production companies: Lichtblick Medien (Berlin) and Atelier Documentaire (Paris). Subject: How EU regulations on data privacy, food safety, and chemical standards have quietly become the global default. Target: Arte and ZDF. Quote from EP: 'This is the most ambitious European co-production we have undertaken.'

Announcement: Launch of a new AI-powered agricultural advisory platform for smallholder farmers. Company: Shamba Intelligence. Platform name: FarmPulse. What it does: Delivers personalised crop management recommendations via SMS and WhatsApp in 12 African languages, using satellite imagery and local weather data. Launch markets: Kenya, Tanzania, and Ethiopia. Scale at launch: 40,000 pre-registered farmers, partnerships with three national agricultural extension services. Founder: Dr. Nyambura Kamau. Quote from founder: 'Extension officers cover 3,000 farmers each. Our platform puts a personal agronomist in every farmer's pocket.' Quote from partner: 'We have been looking for a tool that works without smartphones and without internet — this is the first one that delivers.' Pricing: Free for farmers; revenue from institutional data partnerships.

Announcement: First edition of a pan-Latin American documentary film festival held entirely on riverboats. Festival name: Festival Flotante. Format: 5-day floating festival travelling the Magdalena River in Colombia, screening 32 documentaries from 11 countries on converted cargo barges with open-air screens. Organiser: Colectivo Río Abierto. Festival director: Valentina Restrepo. Dates: March 14-18, 2027. Highlights: Opening night film is a world premiere about fishermen displaced by dam construction; a Youth Jury composed of riverside community members aged 16-22; masterclasses with filmmakers conducted in Spanish and Portuguese. Quote from director: 'Documentary film belongs to the communities it portrays. We are bringing the screen to the river, not the river to the screen.' Attendance expected: 4,000 across five stops.

Announcement: A media company is laying off 30% of its editorial staff and replacing them with AI-generated content. Company: Meridian Media Group. Details: The company will reduce its newsroom from 120 journalists to 84 and deploy an AI content system for routine reporting including earnings summaries, weather updates, sports results, and local government meeting recaps. The remaining journalists will focus on investigative and feature work. Timeline: Restructuring complete by Q1 2027. CEO: Richard Langley. Quote from CEO: 'This is not a reduction in journalism — it is a reallocation toward the journalism that matters most.' No quote from affected staff or union. Revenue context: The company reported a 14% decline in advertising revenue last year.

Announcement: A Japanese animation production studio is opening its first overseas production facility. Company: Studio Kagerou. New facility: A 200-person animation production hub in Taipei, Taiwan, the studio's first operation outside Japan. Purpose: The facility will handle in-between animation, digital compositing, and background art for the studio's theatrical and streaming productions, while creative direction and key animation remain at the Tokyo headquarters. Investment: ¥1.8 billion ($12M). Opening: September 2027. Context: The expansion follows three consecutive internationally distributed titles and growing demand from global streaming platforms. Studio president: Hiroshi Tanabe. Quote from president: 'Taipei has extraordinary animation talent and a creative culture that shares our commitment to hand-drawn craft. This is a partnership, not outsourcing.' Quote from Taipei facility head, Wei-Lin Chou: 'We are building a team that bridges two of Asia's strongest animation traditions.' Staff plan: 80 artists at launch, scaling to 200 within 18 months.
